name: Web Reliability Conformance Demo
summary: The runnable proof of the CustomRecoveryHandler contract (#1051) in a real browser: a handler inspects an error + its RecoveryContext and either declines (null — the next handler is tried) or owns the recovery with a RecoveryResult (retry / queued / fallback / abort). The contract is type-only (reliability/contract.ts); the HTTP-retry / circuit-breaker / offline-queue handlers and the customRecovery registry are impl in Frontier UI, so the demo supplies its own in-demo offline-retry handler. A runtime-conformance section asserts each invariant live: declines an unrecognized error, retries a transient error with backoff, queues an offline error for replay, aborts at the attempt cap, grows the backoff with attempts, and exposes a stable priority key.
description:
Concept

The runnable proof of the CustomRecoveryHandler contract (#1051). A handler inspects an error + its RecoveryContext and either declines (null — the next handler is tried) or owns the recovery with a RecoveryResult (retry/queued/fallback/abort).

In-demo handler, not the FUI impl

The contract is type-only (reliability/contract.ts); the HTTP-retry / circuit-breaker / offline-queue handlers and the customRecovery registry are impl in Frontier UI. So this demo supplies its own in-demo offline-retry handler to prove the contract is realizable.

Verified contract

The runtime-conformance section asserts each invariant live: decline of an unrecognized error, retry-with-backoff for a transient error, offline queueing for replay, attempt-cap abort, exponential backoff growth, and a stable priority key.
